name: honeypot
description: a honeypot attacker-interaction world; actions probe, engage, alert

card.description: a honeypot attacker-interaction world; actions probe, engage, alert

rules: 'probe' gathers the most intel (+2) and deepens engagement (+1) but reaching back toward the adversary raises their suspicion (+2).
'engage' feeds convincing decoys: engagement +2, intel +1, one more exposed service, and suspicion -1 (it looks legitimate).
At engagement >= 7 the adversary believes they have compromised the box ('compromised' becomes true).
'alert' notifies the SOC: sets 'defender_alerted', increments 'alerts_raised', and is invisible to the adversary so suspicion is unchanged. It stays usable even after the session ends.
When suspicion reaches 8 the adversary disconnects and the session ends; probe/engage then have no effect.
'threat_level' is derived each tick from engagement (and compromise): low < medium(>=2) < high(>=5) < critical(>=8 or compromised).
All bounded counters are clamped to [0, 10].

emit.report: Honeypot incident @ step {step}: threat={threat_level}, engagement={attacker_engagement}/10, intel={intel_collected}, suspicion={suspicion}/10, session_active={session_active}, compromised={compromised}, alerts={alerts_raised}.
